commitMessage:
Cannot configure Null annotations for workspace (#2567)

+ when configuring workspace preferences:
  + search for annotations with workspace scope
+ report not found annotation types
+ report duplicate annotation types of different kind (ambiguous)
+ cascade warning dialogs:
  - first report problems (not found / ambiguous)
  - only then check for incompatibilities
+ make detection more robust (single element, semi-qualified name)

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2303
summary: This commit refactors internal preference handling for null annotations, improving search and reporting for annotation types, which is not a general code cleanup.

commitMessage:
Removed unnecessary @SuppressWarnings("deprecation") from jdt.ui

See https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/issues/4553
summary: This commit removes `@SuppressWarnings("deprecation")` related to `java.util.Observable` and `java.util.Observer` when they are deemed unnecessary, likely due to compilation against Java 8 or earlier.

commitMessage:
Adjust highlighting of deprecation (#2578)

After https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/pull/4564
deprecation no longer propagates from a type to its members.

Still highlight as deprecated constructors of anonymous classes and
default constructors.

Fixes https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2579
summary: Internal logic change, not a user-code transformation.

commitMessage:
Fix pasting into a String to not do tabs to spaces formatting (#2576)

- add new JavaTabsToSpacesConverter class which accounts for the
  location of the offset being in a String
- set new converter in CompilationUnitEditor and JavaEditor
- add new JavaPasteTest to JdtTextTextSuite
- fixes #2546
dslRule:
/*!
<!id: replace-tabs-to-spaces-converter>
<!description: Replace TabsToSpacesConverter with JavaTabsToSpacesConverter for Java-specific handling.
<!severity: INFO>
*/
new org.eclipse.jface.text.TabsToSpacesConverter()
=>
new org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.javaeditor.JavaTabsToSpacesConverter()
;;
summary: Replaces `new TabsToSpacesConverter()` with `new JavaTabsToSpacesConverter()`.

commitMessage:
Refactor TypeParametersCleanUp to jdt.core.manipulation (#2581)

- refactor to TypeParametersCleanUpCore for use by jdt.ls
- modify LocalCorrectionBaseSubProcessor and
  LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor appropriately
- modify plugin.xml to use new core clean-up
dslRule:
/*!
<!id: refactor-typeparameterscleanup>
<!description: Refactor TypeParametersCleanUp to TypeParametersCleanUpCore.
<!severity: INFO>
*/
new org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.fix.TypeParametersCleanUp($args$)
=>
new org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.fix.TypeParametersCleanUpCore($args$)
;;
summary: Replaces `new TypeParametersCleanUp($args$)` with `new TypeParametersCleanUpCore($args$)`.

commitMessage:
Highlight deprecation of canonical record constructor (#2582)

Fix highlighting of canonical constructors of a deprecated record type.

See additional observation in https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/pull/2578
summary: Internal logic change, not a user-code transformation.

commitMessage:
Support new warning COMPILER_PB_MEMBER_OF_DEPRECATED_TYPE (#2587)

* Support new warning COMPILER_PB_MEMBER_OF_DEPRECATED_TYPE
  + Surface the option in the severities UI
  + Integrate in quickfix addDeprecatedAnnotationProposal()

* adjust tests to changes in jdt.core

See https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/issues/4568
dslRule:
<!id: add.deprecated.to.method.of.deprecated.type>
<!description: Add @Deprecated to a method if its enclosing class is deprecated but the method is not. This aligns with the COMPILER_PB_MEMBER_OF_DEPRECATED_TYPE warning.
>
<!severity: WARNING>

$retType $methodName($params$) :: containsAnnotation("java.lang.Deprecated") && !isDeprecated($methodName) && !isInConstructor()
=> @java.lang.Deprecated $retType $methodName($params$)
;;

commitMessage:
Refactor StringCleanUp to jdt.core.manipulation (#2590)

- move StringCleanUp to StringCleanUpCore in jdt.core.manipulation
- move code for adding and removing NLS tags from
  LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor to LocalCorrectionsBaseSubProcessor
- change references to StringCleanUp to StringCleanUpCore
summary: Internal class renaming and refactoring of StringCleanUp to StringCleanUpCore within Eclipse JDT UI components.

commitMessage:
Improve fix for COMPILER_PB_MEMBER_OF_DEPRECATED_TYPE (#2592)

Fix invocation as multi fix from the problem hover
+ the new problem must be enabled when creating the refactoring ast

Amend https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/pull/2587
summary: Adds a new configuration entry for 'COMPILER_PB_MEMBER_OF_DEPRECATED_TYPE' within the Java50CleanUp class's 'get  CompilerOptions()' method, affecting how deprecation warnings are handled by the cleanup tool.

commitMessage:
Refactor UnimplementedCodeCleanUp to jdt.core.manipulation (#2602)

- refactor to UnimplementedCodeCleanUpCore
- modify references appropriately
- add new
  LocalCorrectionsBaseSubProcessor.getUnimplementedMethodsProposals()
  to be used by LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor
dslRule:
<!id: use-unimplemented-code-cleanup-core>
<!description: Replace instantiation of UnimplementedCodeCleanUp with UnimplementedCodeCleanUpCore, reflecting an internal refactoring within JDT UI components.>
<!severity: HINT>

new org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.fix.UnimplementedCodeCleanUp($args$)
=>
new org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.fix.UnimplementedCodeCleanUpCore($args$)
;;
summary: Replaces direct instantiation of `org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.fix.UnimplementedCodeCleanUp` with `org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.fix.UnimplementedCodeCleanUpCore`, reflecting a class name change during an internal JDT refactoring.

commitMessage:
Refactor PatternCleanUp to jdt.core.manipulation (#2608)

- refactor PatternCleanUp to PatternCleanUpCore in jdt.core.manipulation
- change references
summary: Internal class rename for Eclipse JDT framework.

commitMessage:
Refactor a number of clean-ups to jdt.core.manipulation (#2609)

- refactor cleanups from A-B to jdt.core.manipulation
- modify references

Refactor more clean-ups to jdt.core.manipulation (#2610)

- move more clean-ups to jdt.core.manipulation
- modify references

Propose adding JUnit 6 to classpath when creating a JUnit Jupiter test case

If a Java project has no JUnit container on the classpath,
creating a test case with the respective wizard
will prompt the user to add a JUnit container.

This change adds JUnit 6 to the list of containers in the proposal,
for JUnit Jupiter tests. The list now has JUnit 5 and JUnit 6.

Fixes: #2606

Fix Markdown comments indentation (#2613)

This commit adds an additional space after a markdown start tag

fixes : https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2500
dslRule:
<!id: markdown.javadoc.indentation.fix>
<!description: Adds a space after '///' in markdown Javadoc comments to ensure correct indentation in the Javadoc auto-indent strategy.
This rule specifically targets the JavaDocAutoIndentStrategy class to avoid unintended side effects on other string literals.
>
<!severity: INFO>

$buf.append("///") :: inClass("org.eclipse.jdt.internal.ui.text.javadoc.JavaDocAutoIndentStrategy")
=> $buf.append("/// ")
;;
summary: Adds a trailing space to the string literal "///" when passed to an append method, specifically within the JavaDocAutoIndentStrategy class to correct markdown comment indentation.

commitMessage:
Ensure asterisk added between sequential upper case letters (#2621)

* Ensure asterisk added between sequential upper case letters

- modify FilteredTypesSelectionDialog.getPatternText() to ensure
  that asterisks are added between sequential upper-case letters
  unless the string is entirely upper-case
- fixes #2583
summary: This commit changes the logic for adding asterisks in pattern text. The specific transformation in `canAddAnyStringNext` (`!Character.isUpperCase(c) && c != '.' && c != '*'` to `c != '.' && c != '*'`) is a semantic change rather than a mere cleanup or simplification applicable across different contexts.

commitMessage:
Add markdown code templates and way to specify to use them (#2615)

* Add markdown code templates and way to specify to use them

- add new preference constant CODEGEN_USE_MARKDOWN
- add new markdown comment templates to default-templates.properties
- add new Markdown Comments category to the CodeTemplateBlock class
  which displays the new markdown comment templates
- add new CodeGenerationContextTypes for new templates
- modify StubUtility methods that create comments to use the new
  templates if markdown should be used (new option on and JVM 23+)
- add new GenerateGettersSettersTest23 to show new behavior
- add new tests to JavadocQuickFixTest25 where new comments get
  added to show they honor the new preference
- fixes #2554

React to 3 new warnings after compiler bugfix (#2632)

New warnings were reported since https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.core/issues/4579

+ escalate one deprecation to "forRemoval" as the method can no longer exist when the terminally deprecated class DefaultPartitioner is removed
+ add @SuppressWarnings("forRemoval")

See https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/pull/2632
summary: Cannot create a reusable DSL rule for adding context-dependent annotation attributes or for guarding on annotation attribute values.

commitMessage:
More refactoring of LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor methods (#2642)

* More refactoring of LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor methods

- move methods to LocalCorrectionsBaseSubProcessor
- move one method from QuickAssistProcessor that
  LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor calls and have QuickAssistProcessor
  use it instead
- add new constructors where needed
summary: Refactoring static method bodies to delegate to instance methods and cross-class method delegation is beyond the scope of the TriggerPattern DSL.

commitMessage:
Revert "More refactoring of LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor methods (#2642)" (#2645)

This reverts commit 0460f877d653cb0c0060194590e894a729777a6f.
summary: The commit reverts a refactoring that extracted proposal creation logic into core classes. It restores the original, more complex, direct implementation of several methods within LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor and QuickAssistProcessor, and removes associated core classes and their conversion methods/constructors. This is a large-scale structural change, not a local code cleanup expressible by the DSL.

commitMessage:
Do not offer to add type arguments where diamond operator would do (#2669)

* Do not offer to add type arguments where diamond operator would do

- modify LocalCorrectionsSubProcessor.addTypeArgumentsFromContext()
  to not modify unless the node is the type of a variable declaration
  or the left side of an assignment or a single variable declaration
- add new test to LocalCorrectionsQuickFixTest
- version bump
- fixes #2668

---------

Co-authored-by: Eclipse JDT Bot <jdt-bot@eclipse.org>
dslRule:
<!id: use-diamond-operator>
<!description: Replace explicit type arguments with the diamond operator where type can be inferred by the compiler (Java 7+).
This transformation is only applied if the compiler infers the same type with the diamond operator, ensuring semantic equivalence.>
<!severity: HINT>

new $Type<$Exprs$>()
=>
new $Type<>()
;;
summary: Replaces explicit type arguments in constructor calls (e.g., `new ArrayList<String>()`) with the diamond operator (`new ArrayList<>()`) if type inference allows for identical type derivation. This is a common Java 7+ modernization.

commitMessage:
Enable Javadoc settings when Markdown formatting switched on (#2634)

The Javadoc formatting section was incorrectly disabled when "Enable
Classic Javadoc formatting" was unchecked, even if Markdown Javadoc
formatting was enabled. Now it remains active when either option is
selected.

Fixes : https://github.com/eclipse-jdt/eclipse.jdt.ui/issues/2630
summary: The changes are structural additions and modifications of lambda logic, not simple expression replacements.

commitMessage:
Fix NPE in EnumConstructorTargetFinder (#2690) (#2691)

* Fix NPE in EnumConstructorTargetFinder (#2690)
summary: Cannot express adding an 'if' null check block.

commitMessage:
Making the dialog area of NewTypeWizard pages scrollable (#2653)

* Making the dialog area of NewTypeWizard pages scrollable

When the wizard dialog is manually resized or during DPI change, text on NewTypeWizard pages can become partially cut off without any visual indication. This update makes the page area scrollable, so any content that would otherwise be cut off remains accessible to the user through scrollbars.
summary: Cannot express adding a new method and performing subsequent structural UI changes.

commitMessage:
enable folding with "Only show selected Java element" (#2302)

* enable folding with "Only show selected Java element" #2264

- move the enablement to replace System.getProperty() for

Fix javadoc warnings in ISharedImages

Stop pointing to deprecated Cursor constructors in
https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.swt/pull/2563
summary: This commit updates a Javadoc @see reference from a deprecated Cursor constructor to a newer one. This is a documentation-only change and falls outside the scope of code transformations supported by the TriggerPattern DSL.

Add a command to persist workbench state
